order dated 24.09.2018 on the ground that the respondent’s name 
appears at sl. no. 22 in the list of eligible candidates, while those at sl. 
nos. 23 and 28 in the same list were appointed. The Court reasoned 
that since those who were relatively less meritorious were considered 
favourably pursuant to the High Court’s direction in a separate writ 
petition, the respondent must be treated at par with them as he is in 
a relatively better position. Hence, the Court directed the appellant 
to consider the respondent’s case for appointment to the post of 
LDC or any other suitable post or any suitable supernumerary post 
in the same manner as the other case. The appellant’s writ appeal 
came to be dismissed by the order impugned herein, on a similar 
546 
reasoning that the respondent’s case must be treated at par with 
the other appointed candidates as he is higher in the list of eligible 
candidates.
